home.title: Strategy Guides, Tier Lists & Tools
home.description: The reference wiki for Foundation: Galactic Frontier. Strategy guides, hero tier lists, flagship decks, ground teams, event walkthroughs, gift codes and in-depth tools, in English and French.

guides.description: In-depth Foundation: Galactic Frontier guides covering early, mid and late game progression, combat tactics, economy and resource management.

hero.subtitle_1: The ultimate field guide to Foundation: Galactic Frontier.
hero.subtitle_2: Strategies, tips, and data to conquer the galaxy.

tips.t1_content: Prioritize story missions early on. They unlock critical features like recruitment, ship modules, and new game modes essential for progression.
tips.t2_content: Claim your daily rewards every single day. Unclaimed resources are missed opportunities for growth that you effectively cannot recover.
tips.t3_content: Join an active guild as soon as possible. You gain access to shared resources, coordinated battles, and guild-exclusive events that boost your progress.
tips.t4_content: Master the three core styles: Beam beats Kinetic, Kinetic beats Ion, and Ion beats Beam. Mixing these effectively is key to winning battles.
tips.t5_content: Skills activate from left to right. Place your support or debuff champions in the first slots to maximize the damage of your attackers.
tips.t6_content: In combat, always pay attention to telegraphs. Avoid red attack zones to preserve your fleet's health.
tips.t7_content: Efficiently gather Ice Shards, Metal Shards, and Food. Upgrade your Mothership's Energy Core to boost generation rates.
tips.t8_content: Establish trade routes as soon as they are available. They provide a stable income of coins and materials passiveley.
tips.fleet_power_desc: Victory doesn't come down to firepower alone - it's about knowing how to use it. Understand Energy Types, Champion Synergy, and Flagship Styles.
tips.fleet_power_intro: In the vast reaches of the Galactic Frontier, victory doesn't come down to firepower alone - it's about knowing how to use it. Every ship in your fleet operates with a distinct Energy Type and can be further enhanced through Champion synergy and your Flagship's Style. Understanding these systems is key to mastering the battlefield.
tips.fleet_power_energy_types_txt: Each ship in Foundation: Galactic Frontier specializes in one of three Energy Types: Beam, Kinetic, or Ionic. These Energy Types interact with one another in a strategic counter system that determines combat effectiveness.
tips.fleet_power_beam_txt:
Uses concentrated energy beams to pierce defenses.
Strong against: Kinetic ships
Weak against: Ionic ships
Battle Role: Precision and consistency - Beam ships excel at breaking through enemy armor with sustained damage.
tips.fleet_power_kinetic_txt:
Fires solid projectiles for massive impact.
Strong against: Ionic ships
Weak against: Beam ships
Battle Role: High burst damage and reliable physical attacks that can quickly crush weakened foes.
tips.fleet_power_ionic_txt:
Manipulates charged particles to disrupt systems and weaken enemies.
Strong against: Beam ships
Weak against: Kinetic ships
Battle Role: Control and disruption - Ionic ships excel at interfering with enemy performance, reducing their combat potential.
tips.fleet_power_ionic_tip: Combat Tip: Using a ship strong against your target's Energy Type grants a 5% damage boost - a small edge that can decide close battles!
tips.fleet_power_synergy_hdr: Champion Synergy - Aligning Heroes and Fleets
tips.fleet_power_synergy_txt: Your fleet's strength isn't just about the ships - it's also about who commands them. Each Champion has an Energy Type that corresponds to the ships they lead. Matching your Champions' Energy Types with their assigned ships creates powerful synergy bonuses.
tips.fleet_power_stats_txt:
ATK (Attack): Increases damage output.
DEF (Defense): Reduces incoming damage.
INT (Intelligence): Boosts tactical and ability efficiency (affecting damage, healing, or defense depending on the Champion's role).
tips.fleet_power_stats_tip: Pro Tip: Align your fleet's Energy Type with your Champions to maximize overall combat efficiency. A properly synchronized lineup can turn the tide of any engagement.
tips.fleet_power_flagship_txt: Your Flagship serves as the command center of your fleet - and its Core Components define your fleet's overall Style. Managing and upgrading these components allows you to adapt your strategy to different opponents.
tips.fleet_power_core_txt: The Core Component determines your Flagship's damage Style - and by extension, your entire fleet's. Changing Core Components lets you counter enemy Styles, granting a 5% damage bonus when you have the advantage. This adaptability can create strong tactical edges during key encounters.
tips.fleet_power_components_txt:
Additional Flagship Components further enhance your ships' base attributes (ATK, DEF, INT). Higher-quality components offer greater boosts. Components can be obtained through:
- The Glory Shop
- Ascendancy Shrines
- Tribute Vessels
tips.fleet_power_components_tip: Upgrade Tip: Regularly upgrading your Flagship's components ensures your fleet remains competitive and scales with more challenging enemies.
tips.fleet_power_formation_txt: Tactics matter - and so does the order of activation. When deploying your Champions, remember that skills activate in the order they're placed, from left to right in your formation. Adjusting this order can dramatically influence how battles unfold.
tips.fleet_power_formation_tip: Strategic Example: Place support or debuff Champions first so their skills activate before your main damage dealers unleash their attacks.
tips.fleet_power_final_txt: To truly command the Galactic Frontier, mastery of Energy Types, Champion synergy, and Flagship Styles is essential. Experiment with combinations, adapt your formations, and use the Energy counter system to your advantage. Whether you favor precise Beam strikes, overwhelming Kinetic barrages, or disruptive Ionic tactics - your fleet's potential is only as strong as your strategic choices.
tips.port_occ_desc: Port Occupation as the core of guild power and trade networks.
tips.port_occ_intro: Port Occupation as the core of guild power and trade networks.
tips.port_occ_declaring_hdr: Declaring War: The Beginning of Conquest
tips.port_occ_declaring_txt:
Only Commerce Guild Chairmen and Executive Managers can declare war.
Rules for Planet Ports: Must hold a Star Port in the same/adjacent system; exception: Level 1 Planet Ports.
Rules for Star Ports: Must already control a Planet Port in the same system.
Multiple guild declarations: battle time = earliest declaration.
Declaration Phase countdown mechanic applies.
tips.port_occ_battle_txt:
There is a 1-hour window to begin the assault after the declaration ends.
Goal: Defeat garrison forces in the Port's Space Station.
Final blow to Fortress HP = victory.
Post-battle protection period applies.
tips.port_occ_rewards_txt:
Resource Buffs for all guild members.
Participation Rewards.
First Occupation Rewards (exclusive honor bonus).
Home Port Buff (Trader's Home Port).
Stellar Halo (global guild buff from Star Ports).
Overlord System: 5 positions, 10% ATK bonus, one per quadrant.
tips.port_occ_home_port_claim:
Claim instructions:
- Daily Rewards: Complete Trade Network Events
- Resource Earnings: Guild → Ports → Claim Button
tips.port_occ_star_port_txt:
20% trade bonus (TA) when trading to that system.
Stellar Halo buff (guild-wide, no stacking for duplicate star levels).
tips.port_occ_losing_txt: Losing control offers a strategic reflection and an opportunity to reorganize.
tips.port_occ_strategy_txt:
Max 24 Ports per guild (16 Planet + 8 Star).
Limited daily War Declarations.
Guild Chat coordination is highly emphasized.
tips.port_occ_final_txt: Port Occupation is a true test of foresight, teamwork, and timing.
tips.port_occ_credit: Credit: Special thanks to Mod Liannin.
tips.home_port_desc: Understand your Home Port: the cornerstone of your trading empire in the Galactic Frontier.
tips.home_port_intro: Every Trader needs a reliable base of operations. In Foundation: Galactic Frontier, your Home Port is exactly that: the hub from which you launch fleets, collect rewards, and grow your trading power across the stars.
tips.home_port_purpose_hdr: What Is the Purpose of a Home Port?
tips.home_port_purpose_txt:
Traders can settle in a Planet Port and set it as their Home Port. Once settled, several key features unlock:

• Settle-in Buff: Gain a set of powerful benefits tied to your chosen location.
• Instant Fleet Recall: Teleport your entire fleet to your Home Port's vicinity at any time.
• Automatic Redeployment: If your fleet is destroyed, it respawns near your Home Port.

Trader Commissions also refresh in the star system of your Home Port. As you level it up, you unlock:

• Higher-quality trade commissions
• Increased trade capacity
• Expanded fleet reach

Upgrading your Home Port directly increases your progression potential.
tips.home_port_settlement_txt:
Traders can set their Home Port on any Planetary Port, including those owned by their guild. Key rules:

• Each Trader can only have one active Home Port at a time.
• You can choose a Neutral Port in your starting system, or a Port occupied by your Guild.

To settle:
1. Tap your chosen Port on the Star System map.
2. Tap 'Port' to settle in.

To locate your Home Port quickly, tap the Home Port icon on the map. Tap Recall to teleport your fleet back. Teleports regenerate every 15 minutes.
tips.home_port_settlement_note: Note: The Port level you can settle in depends on your Energy Core level.
tips.home_port_neutral_txt:
Neutral Ports are unclaimed planets available to any trader or guild. Key facts:

• Only appear in systems up to Level 5.
• Do not generate Resource Earnings for any guild.
• Ideal for independent traders or beginners.

Beyond Level 5, settling requires being in a guild that already holds Ports in that system.
tips.home_port_nonneutral_txt:
Planetary Ports under guild control come with major strategic advantages:

• Contribute Resource Earnings to your guild.
• Add Occupation Points toward Guild Rankings.
• Unlock progressively through Trade Era cycles.
• With research, a guild can hold up to 16 Planetary Ports.
tips.home_port_nonneutral_tip: Pro Tip: Maintain at least one Planetary Port in every system where your guild controls a Star Port: this maximizes efficiency and resource earnings.
tips.home_port_starport_txt:
Star Ports are high-value strategic assets, but they work differently from Planet Ports:

• Always open for occupation and war declarations.
• Provide major trade capability buffs to your guild.
• Cannot be used as Home Ports: only garrisoned.

Occupation requirements:
• Neutral Port: Must be in your starting region, or your guild must already hold Ports there.
• Ports above Level 5: Requires being in a guild with either a conquered non-neutral Port nearby, or a Planetary Port in the same system.
• Star Port: Your guild must already have territory in that system.
tips.home_port_buffs_txt:
Your Home Port is more than a safe zone: it actively amplifies your performance. Depending on port level, you receive boosts to:

• Trade Attempts (TA): The number of trade trips your flagship can make.
• Building Speed (BS): Reduces time needed for upgrades and construction.
• Daily Reward (DR): Improves the quality of your daily chest, matching your port level.

How to claim your rewards:
• Daily Rewards: Complete Trade Network Events.
• Resource Earnings: Navigate to Guild → Ports → Claim Button.
tips.home_port_final_txt:
• Keep your Home Port leveled and active, as it directly defines your growth potential.
• Join a strong guild to gain access to higher-tier Planetary Ports.
• Balance your strategy: consider when to stay neutral and when to commit to guild cooperation.
• Claim your rewards every day; unclaimed resources are permanently lost opportunities.

Your Home Port is the heart of your trading empire. Choose it wisely, upgrade it strategically, and command your fleets with confidence!
tips.home_port_credit: Based on the official Foundation: Galactic Frontier guide. Special thanks to Mod Liannin.
tips.vip_program_title: Unlocking the Secrets of the FGF GTC Exclusive VIP Program
tips.vip_program_desc: How to get invited to the elite, invite-only GTC Exclusive VIP program in Foundation: Galactic Frontier.
tips.vip_program_intro:
If you have spent any significant amount of time traversing the universe of Foundation: Galactic Frontier (FGF), you might have heard whispers of an elite, invite-only club: the GTC Exclusive VIP program.

For many players, gaining access to this premium service (and getting to interact with dedicated GTC Service representatives like Stella) is a major goal. But how exactly does one get an invitation to this exclusive circle?

We reached out directly to the GTC Service team to get the official scoop for the fgfwiki.com community. Here is everything you need to know about how the GTC selects its lucky VIP players.
tips.vip_program_what_is_hdr: What is the GTC Exclusive VIP Program?
tips.vip_program_what_is_txt: The GTC Exclusive VIP program is a specialized service designed to reward the most dedicated commanders in Foundation: Galactic Frontier. Players who are selected for this program gain access to personalized support and an enhanced gaming experience tailored to their journey.
tips.vip_program_how_chosen_txt:
Unlike a standard subscription that you can simply purchase, the GTC Exclusive VIP status is strictly invite-only. According to official GTC Service representatives, the selection process is not based on a single metric. Instead, the system evaluates a comprehensive set of factors for each merchant and player.

If you want to increase your chances of being selected, here are the core areas you need to focus on:

1. Daily Activity
Consistency is key. The GTC looks for commanders who are logging in regularly and actively participating in the FGF universe day after day.

2. Main Quest Progress
You cannot just idle in the game; you need to push the boundaries of the frontier! Advancing through the Main Quest shows the GTC that you are deeply invested in the game's lore, mechanics, and long-term progression.

3. In-Game Behavior
Your overall conduct within the game matters. Engaging positively with the game's systems, participating in events, and demonstrating healthy in-game behavior contributes to your overall profile as a potential VIP candidate.

4. Spending History
As with many premium gaming services, your investment in the game plays a role. While it is not the only factor, your purchasing history with in-game merchants is taken into account when the GTC evaluates your account for VIP eligibility.
tips.vip_program_takeaway_txt: Becoming a GTC Exclusive VIP is not about finding a shortcut; it is about being a dedicated, active, and progressing member of the Foundation: Galactic Frontier community. Keep pushing forward in your main quests, log in daily, and support the game, and you might just be the next lucky player to receive that coveted message from the GTC Service team!
tips.vip_program_perks_txt:
Once you are admitted into this select group, you can expect several exclusive benefits to enhance your gaming experience. Here are the primary membership perks:

1. Birthday Benefits
You will receive exclusive birthday wishes and special gifts on your birthday.

2. Monthly Check-In
Contact Stella by the 15th of each month and send the message "Let's FOUNDATION" to check in. Your monthly benefits will be delivered to you by the 20th.

3. Holiday Celebrations
Special holiday gifts are prepared for Master Traders during major festive periods throughout the year.

4. Exclusive Support
Enjoy direct access to Stella for quick responses and prompt assistance during working hours, ensuring faster resolution of any issues you encounter.

5. More VIP Perks
Additional features and exclusive rewards are planned for the future.
tips.migration_title: Interstellar Migration Update: Future Waves and Current Rules
tips.migration_desc: Everything you need to know about future waves of the Interstellar Migration system and the current transfer rules.
tips.migration_intro:
The first two waves of the Interstellar Migration system in Foundation: Galactic Frontier have officially concluded, serving as an initial testing phase for the community. With many players and guild leaders actively trying to organize their servers for upcoming transfers, the most common question right now is when the next migration window will open.

Here is the latest information regarding the future of server migrations, along with a complete breakdown of the currently known transfer rules to help you prepare.
tips.migration_status_txt:
At this time, there is no exact schedule for the next wave of migrations. The development team is currently analyzing the results of the first two server transfers and actively collecting feedback from the participating players.

The primary goal of this evaluation period is to optimize future transfer arrangements. By reviewing the data from the test phases, the developers aim to ensure a smoother and more balanced migration process. While you wait for official in-game updates regarding the schedule, it is highly recommended to familiarize yourself with the core migration mechanics so your guild is ready to act.
tips.migration_how_works_txt:
The game categorizes servers into three distinct strength tiers, calculated using combined active trader power and highest Warzone performance. Players are similarly divided into four power tiers based on their historical peak power.

To rebalance the galactic map, weaker target servers are allowed to absorb a higher number of powerful players. Each destination server has strict quotas for incoming players:
tips.migration_reqs_txt:
To successfully migrate, a player must possess 10 Migration Tickets and meet all of the following conditions:

• Idle Status: All flagships must be in an idle state.
• No Guild Affiliation: The player must not be a member of any Commerce Guild. Leaving your guild is a mandatory technical step required by the system.
• No Official Titles: The player must not hold any official title, including Trader Prince, Trader Conqueror, or Migration Officer.
• Cooldown Period: A minimum of 25 days must have passed since the player's last migration.
• No Duplicate Characters: The player cannot have an existing character on the target server.
• Single Application: Only one active migration application is permitted at a time.
tips.migration_effects_txt:
Transferring to a new server will impact your data and progress.

Data Reset:
• Your Home Port will relocate to a random respawn point in a starting region on the new server.
• Server and Arena rankings will be completely cleared.
• All in-game mail will be deleted.
• Urgent and Regular trade orders will be paused, but this will not consume your attempts.

Data Preserved:
• Group chats and private messages remain intact.
• Standard resources are kept, provided they do not carry system recovery warnings.
• Previously discovered planets and systems remain explored.
tips.migration_stages_txt: When a migration window officially opens, it follows four distinct phases. During the event, the top 10 Commerce Guilds on every server are permitted to post recruitment advertisements at the Migration Relay Station to attract incoming players. We will continue to monitor the situation and update the community as soon as the next schedule is released.
tips.combat_math_desc: Uncover how space combat calculates damage, counterattacks, command points, and why visuals lie.
tips.combat_math_timeline_txt: In serious PvP, maxed ships operate on a strict 1-second timeline. Every second, you fire 1 normal attack and the enemy fires 1. Because you hit them, you take counterattack damage, and vice versa. Champion abilities trigger exactly at second 4, and then every 9 seconds in a fixed order.
tips.combat_math_stats_txt: ATK scales basic strikes, counterattacks, and skills labeled with ATK. INT determines ability output for both champions and ships. DEF reduces incoming damage but has no effect on outgoing damage. Note that testing reveals Cocoon's actual shielding averages 110-150% of DEF in combat, contradicting the in-game tooltip.
tips.combat_math_cp_dr_txt: Damage Reduction (DR) represents the most effective defensive attribute as it mitigates all incoming sources of damage. Command Points (CP) introduce major power swings: having superior CP relative to your opponent increases your damage and grants bonus DR, making CP upgrades a high priority (a 20 CP lead provides about a 15% swing).
tips.gungir_guide_desc: Master the Gungir, the ultimate flagship for energy control, tempo disruption, and suppression.
tips.gungir_guide_overview_txt: Gungir is a premium flagship designed to control combat pacing. Rather than delivering high burst damage or healing, it optimizes energy dynamics by generating +720 energy per rotation for your fleet while reducing enemy energy recovery by a flat 300 points.
tips.gungir_guide_mechanics_txt: Reducing enemy energy delays their skill activations while accelerating your own champion rotations, which can significantly increase your team's ability frequency. Gungir also gains stacking combat bonuses that can scale up to a 25% increase during extended encounters.
tips.gungir_guide_deployment_txt: For high-investment players, configuring Gungir for Kinetic damage (paired with Killer Bee) and deploying Gram for Beam is optimal. Non-whales should retain Gram on Kinetic as their primary combat option and assign Gungir to Beam as a secondary ship.
tips.prismatic_cores_desc: How to handle flagship breakthroughs and the pity system of the cosmic slot machine.
tips.prismatic_cores_overview_txt: Once flagship modules reach their level cap, breakthroughs are required to progress. Breakthroughs cost Prismatic Cores, which are rare and highly valuable.
tips.prismatic_cores_attempt_txt: Each upgrade cycle starts with a base success rate of 4% per attempt, backed by a safety count that guarantees success on the 40th try. As upgrade levels increase, success rates decline and the resource requirements per attempt rise.
tips.prismatic_cores_strategy_txt: Due to steep upgrade cost scaling, avoid focusing on a single flagship to level 9 immediately. Instead, adopt a balanced progression: elevate Gram modules to level 3, then Opportunity to 3, and then Demerzel to 3, before repeating the cycle for levels 6 and 9. Try to align breakthroughs with GvG or Top 100 events to secure points.
tips.guild_vouchers_desc: How to farm vouchers efficiently and buy high-value items like Beacons and Speedups.
tips.guild_vouchers_overview_txt: Guild Vouchers represent a stable, high-value currency. They should be prioritized in the Guild Store for acquiring Multidimensional Beacons and research speedups, which are vital assets for late-game progression.
tips.guild_vouchers_farm_txt: Boost your weekly voucher earnings by exceeding standard daily limits. Participating in up to 10 additional Shrine battles daily provides 2,500 vouchers per run, while completing up to 10 extra Holy Tribute Vessel runs yields 3,000 vouchers each.
tips.resource_priority_desc: Identify high-value purchases and avoid traps selling passive space dust.
tips.resource_priority_standard_txt: Avoid purchasing bundles containing basic resources such as Food, Metal, Water, or standard Coins. Early-game Food shortages disappear quickly, while Metal and Water naturally reach massive surplus states as your account develops, making these packages poor investments.
tips.resource_priority_premium_txt: Focus on bundles containing rare materials like Potential Crystals, Flagship Blueprints, or event-specific boosters. The Monthly Card and the Champion Pass offer the highest value. Save your Credits for the Black Market to purchase speedups exclusively at a 50% discount.
tips.building_priority_desc: Upgrade order to optimize construction times and flagship utility.
tips.building_priority_critical_txt: The Crew Cabins (which reduce all construction times) and Cargo Holds (which increase trade rewards) should be prioritized. Upgrading these early has a compounding positive effect on your base development.
tips.building_priority_important_txt: The Repair Cabins and Research Office are secondary priorities. Weapon systems and engines should only be upgraded when preparing for active conflict. Hangar upgrades offer minimal early-game return and should be delayed until major Core milestones (15, 20, 25, 30).
tips.home_port_repair_desc: Repair your damaged fleets for free without wasting valuable repair modules.
tips.home_port_repair_overview_txt: Vessels assigned to active fleets docked at your Home Port recover durability passively over time without cost, which does not apply to trade route or hangar ships. To conserve repair resources, place damaged Ion or Beam units onto an active flagship for passive healing during automated rallies.
tips.build_queue_trick_desc: Make the most of the 24-hour second construction queue.
tips.build_queue_trick_overview_txt: The secondary construction queue, unlocked for 500 Credits, has a 24-hour duration. Any upgrade initiated before this window closes will proceed to completion even if the project requires multiple days, allowing you to maximize the value of the temporary queue.
tips.star_of_guild_title: Upcoming Feature: Star of the Guild
tips.star_of_guild_desc: Discover the upcoming Guild Star tournament launching on June 28 for Servers 1-16. Qualify with your commerce guild, complete challenges, and boost rewards.
tips.star_of_guild_intro: A new competitive weekly event, Star of the Guild, is launching on June 28 for Servers 1 to 16! Work together with your guild members to earn exclusive bonuses and prove your commercial power.
tips.star_of_guild_qualify_txt: Every Monday at 00:00 UTC, the top 10 Commerce Guilds on the server ranked by power will qualify. Once qualification is complete, rankings are locked for the week.
tips.star_of_guild_rewards_txt: Guild performance and overall active participation during the week determine the rewards. Baseline rewards are shared by all members, but individual efforts of designated Guild Stars can multiply the total prizes.
tips.star_of_guild_leader_txt: The guild leader will have the authority to appoint one outstanding member as the 'Honorable Trader' to represent the guild's success.
tips.migration_tier_high_desc: Destination servers with high active power tiers.
tips.migration_tier_mid_desc: Destination servers with medium active power tiers.
tips.migration_tier_low_desc: Destination servers with lower active power tiers.
tips.migration_stage1_desc: Trader Princes set migration conditions and recruitment messages.
tips.migration_stage2_desc: Eligible players apply and leaders invite prospective members.
tips.migration_stage3_desc: Approved candidates confirm and complete the server transfer.
tips.migration_stage4_desc: The final migration results are processed and made public.

hero_data.h1_notes: Essential for Ion rotations. Huge healing & team-wide INT buffs.
hero_data.h2_notes: Best Kinetic finisher. Deals massive burst damage (200% ATK).
hero_data.h3_notes: Core for Kinetic teams. Lowers enemy DEF by 20% to set up finishers.
hero_data.h4_notes: Beam powerhouse. Piercing damage that scales to 150% ATK.
hero_data.h5_notes: F2P friendly but S-Tier power. Great AOE and ability slowdowns.
hero_data.h6_notes: Beam Sync skill boosts ATK by 15%. Great opener for Beam teams.
hero_data.h7_notes: Ramps team damage by 10% per skill use. Strong anchor for long fights.
hero_data.h8_notes: Incredible shields (20% Max HP) and durability. Top tier tank.
hero_data.h9_notes: Consistent HOT (Heal Over Time) for Beam sustain.

ship_decks.kinetic_meta_desc: The ultimate physical damage setup. Relies on Eva debuffing enemies so Zora can one-shot them.
ship_decks.slot1_reason_kinetic: Lowers enemy DEF and buffs your team's damage BEFORE the big hits land.
ship_decks.slot2_reason_kinetic: High damage dealer. Benefits from Eva's debuffs to crush the enemy.
ship_decks.slot3_reason_kinetic: Provides healing or shields to keep the team alive.
ship_decks.beam_meta_desc: High precision and piercing damage. Melts through Kinetic shields.
ship_decks.slot1_reason_beam: Activates "Beam Sync" immediately, boosting all subsequent beam attacks.
ship_decks.slot2_reason_beam: Keeps the fragile Beam ships alive with heal-over-time.
ship_decks.slot3_reason_beam: Unleashes a massive piercing laser that benefits from Aliya's initial buff.
ship_decks.ion_meta_desc: Control and ability suppression. Excellent for PVP and Bosses.
ship_decks.slot1_reason_ion: Starts the "damage ramp" passive early in the chain.
ship_decks.slot2_reason_ion: Heals the team and provides INT buffs for the finisher.
ship_decks.slot3_reason_ion: Huge AOE explosion that slows enemy skills. Finishes the chain with crowd control.

ground_teams.early_game_desc: Accessible heroes that provide great value until you get Legendaries.
ground_teams.mid_game_desc: Solid team for progression before you unlock the full meta.
ground_teams.late_game_desc: The ultimate meta composition. High synergy and damage output.

events.operation_blackout_desc: Hunt down Tithe Collector fleets across all open star systems, earn Collector Orders, and trigger powerful United Tithe Fleets to compete for top rankings and premium rewards.
events.operation_blackout_golden_rule: Dominion Warzone introduced an economy shift: Credits (CC) have become abundant, making standard Coins the primary bottleneck. Substantial Coin reserves are required for T7 module development and core rank upgrades.
events.operation_blackout_stage_1_desc: Tithe Collectors appear across all open regions. Open the event screen and press SEARCH to instantly locate the nearest one in your current star system.
events.operation_blackout_stage_2_desc: Can be attacked solo, with no rally required. Defeating one rewards both the discoverer and all battle participants equally.
events.operation_blackout_stage_3_desc: After defeating a Tithe Collector, there is a 15% chance to lure a United Tithe Fleet (Boss). Boss fleets cannot be soloed (requires guild rally). They range from LVL 120 to LVL 200 (MAX).
events.operation_blackout_stage_4_desc: Rally participants dealing the most damage to United Tithe Fleets earn ranking points. Initiators earn considerably more points than Participants.
events.operation_blackout_daily_bonus_desc: Cap: 30 per day. Earned by initiating or participating in the defeat of a United Tithe Fleet.
events.operation_blackout_daily_discoverer_desc: Cap: 15 per day. Exclusive to the player who lured out the United Tithe Fleet.
events.operation_blackout_tips_solo_desc: Allocating 1200 energy daily is optimal: use 600 energy to hunt Tithe Collectors for spawning fleets, and reserve the other 600 energy for active rally participation. Organized guild teams will yield the highest developer rewards.
events.operation_blackout_tips_comp_desc: Aim to be the Rally Initiator. Target the highest-level United Tithe Fleets (LVL 170-200). Track your daily caps.
events.operation_blackout_tips_guild_desc: Split the Province by assigning members to specific regions. Set up a rally alert system in guild chat. A coordinated 'trigger squad' can passively farm Discoverer Rewards.
events.operation_blackout_protip1_content: Spend your Collector Orders in the Exchange Shop before the event timer expires, as they may not carry over. Prioritize progression bottlenecks.
events.operation_blackout_protip2_content: Prioritize initiating rallies on high-level fleets for maximum ranking points per battle (e.g. 850 pts for LVL 200 vs 510 pts for Participant).
events.operation_blackout_reward_bonus_value: 18 Orders, 340k Coins, 35.5k Food, 48 Component Mods, 40% chance of 100 Credits
events.operation_blackout_reward_discoverer_val: 78 Orders, 510k Coins, 47.3k Food, 72 Component Mods, 40% chance of 100 Credits
events.kaboom_desc: Defeat waves of robot enemies. AOE damage is the key to success. Goal: 100 kills.
events.kaboom_essentials: In this event, Area of Effect (AOE) damage is the priority. It is the key to success.
events.kaboom_team1_note: Combining Zora (for grouping opponents), Lily (for burst area-of-effect damage), and Jodie (for stacking damage on cluster targets) creates the most efficient composition for clearing waves.
events.kaboom_protip1_content: Boutique recommendation: Prioritize acquiring Weapon Prisms to establish permanent stat advantages, and allocate any remaining currency to purchase Beacons.
events.arms_race_desc: Your guild faces five consecutive waves of a single, powerful training fleet (Drill Cruisers).
events.arms_race_essentials: Voucher Importance: Skipping this event results in a major loss of Guild Vouchers, which are necessary for purchasing items like Beacons and speedups in the updated Guild Store.
events.arms_race_morale_desc: Defeating Tribute Vessels during the cooldown window lowers subsequent wave defenses by up to 30%.
events.arms_race_morale_warning: Claim Morale Chests before Stage A ends, or they will be lost!
events.arms_race_stage_a_location: Appears close to the Guild Hub.
events.arms_race_stage_a_countdown: A 3-minute timer triggers before the first wave.
events.arms_race_stage_a_waves: Initiate attacks via Officer Rallies to secure the +30% damage enhancement. Spread out your highest-damage members across multiple rallies so that developing players can reach their individual scoring milestones.
events.arms_race_stage_a_end: The stage ends after 30 minutes or when the last Drill Cruiser is defeated.
events.arms_race_stage_b_flex: Flexibility: Allows guilds to accommodate members in different time zones.
events.arms_race_stage_b_act: Activation: Must be activated within 24 hours of Stage A’s start.
events.arms_race_stage_b_rewards: Rewards: Rewards are awarded only once, based on your highest performance across either stage.
events.arms_race_stage_b_scale: Scalability: Provides a larger pool of hit points so more members can unlock rewards.
events.battle_trial_golden_rule: Test your tactical strength in this elite individual gauntlet.
events.battle_trial_desc: Scope: Player-focused. Minimum Game Time: Approximately 1 hour.
events.battle_trial_event_page_desc: Click the CHALLENGE button directly within the event interface.
events.battle_trial_quick_access_desc: Select the event icon located on the right side of your viewscreen to start the next challenge.
events.calamity_golden_rule: A weekly gauge of your fleet's raw destructive power.
events.calamity_desc: Scope: Server-wide. 16 rounds total (8 rounds per day, every 3 hours starting at reset). Each round lasts 20 minutes. Rewards: Cocoon fragments and Component Modules.
events.calamity_structure_waves_desc: Each round consists of 10 waves of a single Calamity Ship with 10 seconds between waves.
events.calamity_structure_diff_desc: Ships have increasing hit points. The final ship (Wave 10) has nearly 10 billion HP and is not meant to be defeated, allowing you to deal maximum damage until the round ends.
events.calamity_structure_obj_desc: Defeat at least 5 ships in a single round to claim your daily rewards.
events.calamity_structure_lead_desc: Informative only; there are no extra rewards for leaderboard ranking.
events.calamity_opt_fleet_desc: Go Full Damage: Enemy counter-attack damage is minimal and does not scale. Ignore Defense: Defensive stats and abilities are useless here. Champion Swaps: Swap utility/defensive champions (like Klara, Doug, or Ajita) for pure damage dealers, even if they are only Epic quality.
events.calamity_opt_loc_desc: Hunt Alone: Fighting in a crowd reduces your output by roughly 7%. Solo Spot: Find a location to be alone, usually in lower-level systems, to maximize your damage window. Stay Put: Damage is tracked by location; fighting in different systems simultaneously will not add up your scores.
events.calamity_location_desc: Calamity portals appear in every level 4, 6, 7, and 9 system (9 locations total). Look for the specific red/pink portal icon on the galaxy map to zoom directly to the event.
events.crime_hunt_desc: Main Reward: Jodie Béart Fragments. A full daily run yields 0.9 Jodie Fragments and 135m Universal Speedups.
events.crime_hunt_loop_gather_desc: Complete Commissions or defeat Ascendancy Minions.
events.crime_hunt_loop_exchange_desc: Use Intelligence to get Clues about Gathering.
events.crime_hunt_loop_summon_desc: Use Clues to spawn a Delivery Ark for rallies.
events.crime_hunt_ark_location_desc: Appears in the immediate vicinity of your selected fleet.
events.crime_hunt_ark_combat_desc: Requires a rally costing the initiator 30 AP.
events.crime_hunt_ark_diff_desc: Trivial challenge with 450k HP and zero Command Points.
events.crime_hunt_ark_lifespan_desc: Remains in space for only 30 minutes.
events.crime_hunt_ark_bonus_desc: Defeating an Ark can spawn Scared Tribute Vessels.
events.crime_hunt_opt_capacity_desc: Organize with guildmates to fill every rally to 16 members.
events.crime_hunt_opt_early_desc: Do not use the Early Departure feature to ensure everyone joins.
events.crime_hunt_opt_lvl_desc: Summon Arks in high-level systems to trigger better Sacred Tributes.
events.crime_hunt_opt_hub_desc: Set up next to the Guild Hub or a Jump Gate for faster joining.
events.crime_hunt_opt_limit_desc: You are capped at 5 initiator rewards per day.
events.starry_desc: Main Rewards: Evan Rogers and Killer Bee fragments. A free option is available daily.
events.starry_mech_elite_desc: Exclusive method to unlock and upgrade Elite Legendary Champions Evan Rogers and Killer Bee.
events.starry_mech_acq_desc: Trade Starry Memory items for random champion rewards, including hero fragments.
events.starry_mech_freebie_desc: Receive one free Starry Memory item each day of the event.
events.starry_mech_refresh_desc: Offers to buy additional Starry Memory items refresh on a daily basis.
events.starry_opt_tiers_desc: Cumulative milestones are set at 20, 50, 100, 200, 300, and 500 Starry Memory spent.
events.starry_opt_limit_desc: Rewards last for the whole event and can only be claimed once.
events.starry_opt_eff_desc: Earn an additional 10 fragments for every 100 Starry Memory consumed.
events.starry_opt_gen_desc: Only the first 100 items spent will reward generic fragments.
events.starry_opt_cap_desc: There are no cumulative benefits or rewards for spending beyond the 500 mark.
events.starry_opt_best_desc: Wait until you have accumulated at least 100 Starry Memory before trading them during a single event to maximize your returns.
events.shadowfront_golden_rule: Cross-server tactical dominance. Occupy the Vaults to rule the Outer Rim.
events.shadowfront_desc: Minimum Game Time: 30 minutes. Main Rewards: Component Modules, Guild Vouchers, Speedups, and Valor Coins.
events.shadowfront_req_commerce_desc: Must be established for at least 7 days with 20+ members.
events.shadowfront_req_cap_desc: Maximum of 20 members from each guild can be on the battlefield at once.
events.shadowfront_req_combat_desc: Fleets are not destroyed, but manual repairs are disabled. Defeated fleets respawn at the Outpost with a speed boost.
events.shadowfront_stage1_desc: Open to guilds with the highest power on the server.
events.shadowfront_stage2_desc: Admins assign Squad One and Two (20 mains, 10 substitutes each).
events.shadowfront_stage3_desc: Station your Flagship near a Vault to progress occupation. More fleets = faster capture.
events.shadowfront_stage4_desc: Every 5 minutes, Vaults enter a "Brief Awakening" state, granting massive points to the owner at the end of the 4:30 duration.
events.shadowfront_loc_outpost_desc: Your spawn and respawn point. You cannot be attacked while inside the Outpost range.
events.shadowfront_loc_central_desc: Large continuous points + Massive Awakening bonus.
events.shadowfront_contrib_desc: Earned through combat. Damage translates directly. Occupation grants 5,000 for everyone on the field. Final Hit on a rival’s garrison grants 50,000.
events.shadowfront_signals_desc: Admins appoint 3 Commanders who issue tactical signals (ATTACK, DEFEND, RALLY). Allied participants receive instant notifications and mini-map markers.
events.gvg_warning: ATTENTION: Leaving the guild during the event invalidates all your points.
events.gvg_golden_rule: Resources Management: If you see you cannot reach the next chest tier, STOP. Do not waste items unnecessarily.
events.gvg_desc: The ultimate battle for Star Ports and Territory. Requires high coordination and resource management.
events.gvg_tactic1_content: Go to "Battlefield Showdown" > "View" > "Teleport" to invade enemy territory.
events.gvg_tactic2_content: Use the "Home" button located just above the chat window.
events.gvg_tactic3_content: If enemies are hiding, get points via War Prisms, Port Garrisons, or intercepting Cargo Ships.
events.gvg_daily2_details: Commissions, Promotions, Legendary Skills, Guild Donations.
events.gvg_daily3_details: Research Speedups, Calc Modules, Beacons, Echoes.
events.gvg_daily5_details: Mix of all previous days. MOST RESOURCE HEAVY DAY.
events.gvg_daily6_details: Glory Points (PVP only), Destroy Fleets, Prism Damage/Last Hit.
events.gvg_protip1_content: You only have a 1-hour window. Do not trickle in. Regroup and hit with 7-8 Full Rallies simultaneousy.
events.gvg_protip2_content: Scout the enemy garrison. If they are heavy on Shields (Beam), swap to Kinetic ships.
events.top100_golden_rule: Resource Management: Do nothing without an event. Save for dedicated days.
events.top100_desc: 7-day event crucial for Gram Blueprints. Days 1-5: Development goals. Days 6-7: Galactic Showdown (PVP/Glory).
events.top100_day1_focus: Optimal Milestone Strategy: The cost to secure the fourth milestone chest is disproportionately high compared to its rewards. It is more efficient to stop at the third chest and hold onto rare blueprints or upgrading materials for Guild vs Guild event phases.
events.top100_day1_details:
The goal is to use speedups and buy packs. Using 1 minute of Building, Tech, or Shipbuilding speedups grants 16 points. Buying packs grants 4 points per Credit spent. 

*Strategic Tip:* For new servers, the focus is strictly restricted to Building, Research, and Shipbuilding speedups. Save your repair speedups for later.
events.top100_day2_details:
Earn points by obtaining and promoting champions. A Legendary champion is worth 2000 points, while an Epic is worth 100. Additionally, every 1 point of Power gained via leveling or promoting grants 1 point. 

*Strategic Tip:* On new servers, focus solely on unlocking, leveling up, and promoting champions to maximize your score.
events.top100_day3_details:
Points are awarded for PVE and Trading. Defeating Ascendancy Minions grants 1000-2000 points depending on level. Joining Tribute Vessel rallies grants 8000 points. Completing Trade Commissions awards 2800-4000 points based on rarity. 

*Strategic Tip:* Maximize your stamina usage on Minions and ensure you never miss a Tribute rally.
events.top100_day4_details:
Focus on upgrading your Flagship. Consuming Blueprints grants 2000 points per item. Increasing Flagship Power via upgrades or components grants 1 point per Power gained. 

*Strategic Tip:* This is the best day to use all the Blueprints you have been saving. On new servers, focus specifically on unlocking and promoting Flagship components.
events.top100_day5_details:
This is the "All-in" day. All methods from Days 1, 2, 3, and 4 are active and grant points. 

*Strategic Tip:* If you missed a specific day or have leftover resources, dump them all today to maximize your total rank before the PVP phase begins.
events.top100_day6_details:
The focus shifts entirely to Glory and PVP. 1 Battle Glory point equals 1 event point. 

*Strategic Tip:* You can earn Glory by fighting other players OR by collecting Orbs in the hunting zones (Kanvo, Kruger, Cerno, Sphinx). Fighting in high-level systems (Lvl 4+) grants 100% Glory, while lower systems reduce gains by 50%.
events.top100_tactic1_content: Collect orbs in hunting zones (Kanvo, Kruger, Cerno, Sphinx) or fight in PVP.
events.top100_tactic2_content: Fight in level 4+ systems (Warning: lvl 4-5 systems only give 50% glory).
events.top100_protip1_content: This event is crucial to obtain Gram Flagship Blueprints.
events.top100_protip2_content: Do nothing in-game without an event encouraging it. Save speedups for Day 1, Blueprints for Day 4.
events.top100_protip3_content: Save PVP for "Kill" events. Use Showdown to accumulate enough Glory to clear the shop every two weeks.
events.dominance_desc: Major event revolving around the conquest of the Stellar Fortress Xarnas (Level 9 System).
events.dominance_tactic1_content: Scoring mechanics have been restructured. Points from ship losses and passive damage are now minimal. Instead, focus on earning Glory via active PvP engagements, coordinated star rallies, and maintaining garrison forces at Xarnas.
events.dominance_tactic2_content:
The leader of the conquering guild becomes the Trader Prince. They have 24 hours to keep or transfer the title.
Responsibilities: Distribute Commendations (Gift Boxes), assign Server Titles, and activate Global Boosts.
events.dominance_title1_note: Permanent right hand of the Trader Prince.
events.dominance_protip1_content: You must hold the title AT THE START of the action to get the buff. If you start a construction with the title, the buff remains active even if you lose the title immediately after.
events.dominance_protip2_content: On healthy servers, titles are set to "Auto-accept". This creates a queue where each person has 5 minutes. Join the queue, wait for your turn, launch your upgrades, then let the next person in.
events.dominance_protip3_content:
Internal Affairs: Before new trade routes.
Military: Before ship production batches.
Construction/Research: MANDATORY before long-term upgrades.
events.dominion_war_zone_warning: Energy Core Level 15 minimum is required to jump (Attackers only).
events.dominion_war_zone_desc: Dominion Warzone is a full-scale Server vs. Server (SvS) event that replaces the former Top100 format. Two servers are matched against each other in a week-long competition that culminates in a cross-server invasion on the final day. There is no player cap, so every trader on both servers can participate.
events.dominion_war_zone_schedule_d15_action: Phase 1 - Preparation: Daily objectives earn points to determine Attacker/Defender roles.
events.dominion_war_zone_schedule_d6_action: Phase 2 & 3 - Battle & Contest: Cross-server invasion and fight for the Ascendancy Fortress.
events.dominion_war_zone_phase1_desc: Both servers compete through daily themed objectives to rack up points. The server with the higher point total becomes the Attacker, and the other becomes the Defender. The Attacker gets to invade, while the Defender must hold their ground.
events.dominion_war_zone_phase2_desc: Starts at 00:05 UTC. Attackers jump to the enemy server using the [Enter Battlefield] button, causing a 30-minute cooldown per jump. Defenders stay home to protect their Ascendancy Fortress.
events.dominion_war_zone_phase3_desc: 14:00 to 18:00 UTC. The climax where servers can occupy Garrison Strongholds and the Fortress. First to 100% occupation on the Ascendancy Fortress wins, or Defender wins by default if Attacker doesn't reach 100% by 18:00 UTC.
events.dominion_war_zone_garrison_overview_desc: Fortified structures around the Fortress. During Days 1-5, spawn near your own server's Fortress to garnish and upgrade. On Day 6, the Attacker's Strongholds are teleported to the Defender's Fortress.
events.dominion_war_zone_garrison_types_desc: Offensive: Upgrades reduce cooldown and increase automated damage against enemy fleets (bonuses are permanent). Defensive: Upgrades boost all fleets HP (+20% or +50%) and provide Fleet Damage Reduction (+20%). Defensive bonuses are temporarily lost if occupied by the enemy.
events.dominion_war_zone_garrison_rules_desc: Managed on a server-wide basis. Max rally capacity is set by the first player to station a fleet. Only players holding Trader Prince or Military Counselor titles can reassign the Garrison Leader (when not in combat).
events.dominion_war_zone_anti_lag_desc: Idle fleets not assigned to Auto Rally are temporarily hidden. Attacking fleets left idle for too long on the enemy server are automatically sent home.
events.dominion_war_zone_loss_comp_desc: A portion of lost resources is refunded after cross-server battles. Combat craft destroyed in fights against traders from the opposing server are refunded by 30%, along with corresponding resources and Shipbuilding Speedups.
events.dominion_war_zone_strategy_prep_desc: Every point counts. Coordinate with alliance and server leadership early to push for Attacker or deliberately aim for Defender based on your collective strength.
events.dominion_war_zone_strategy_battle_desc: Upgrade Strongholds before Saturday. Plan your jumps carefully due to 30-min cooldowns. Attackers must push hard (Contest ends at 18:00 UTC). Defenders must protect Defensive Strongholds. Coordinate server-wide rallies and prep your fleets in advance.
events.dominion_war_zone_qr_win_val: First to 100% Fortress occupation (or Defender by default at 18:00 UTC)
events.dominion_war_zone_qr_loss_val: 30% of combat craft + resources + Shipbuilding Speedups
events.dominion_war_zone_qr_leaders_val: Changeable by Trader Prince / Military Counselor only
events.united_frontline_desc: United Frontline is a cooperative cross-server event where traders join forces to take down a supercharged boss: the Immortal Ascendancy Shrine. Access the event through the United Frontline page in the event menu to join a shared instance with multiple servers. Standard Ascendancy Shrines will not appear on the map during the event.
events.united_frontline_schedule_cadence_desc: Every two weeks, on Thursdays and Fridays.
events.united_frontline_schedule_req_desc: Your server must have been open for at least 90 days.
events.united_frontline_reward_assist_value: Every additional takedown grants Assistance Rewards after claiming daily Kill Rewards.
events.united_frontline_phase1_desc: The Shrine starts at full power. Focus on dealing consistent damage, but conserve your cooldowns for the later phases.
events.united_frontline_phase2_desc: Reinforcements are called in. Assign a few traders to prioritize Pilgrim Guards while the rest maintain pressure on the Shrine. Killing them grants 'Concentrated Energy', a buff that increases all traders' damage against the Shrine.
events.united_frontline_phase3_desc: The Shrine deploys a protective structure that massively reduces incoming damage. You must destroy the Faith Barrier first. Once broken, the Shrine enters a 'Weakened' state where it takes increased damage, so use your burst damage here.
events.united_frontline_tactic_respawn_desc: After the Immortal Ascendancy Shrine is defeated, it respawns after 1 minute, creating a continuous farming loop. Use the 'Request Assistance' feature to call for reinforcements from other traders in the instance.
events.united_frontline_tactic_rating_desc: Your performance is tracked via a Battle Rating based on your DPS. The trader who ranks first earns the 'Strongest Hunter' title for the next round.
events.united_frontline_protip_strategy_desc:
1. Start steady: conserve cooldowns in Phase 1.
2. Kill the Guards: Concentrated Energy is your damage multiplier.
3. Break the Barrier: don't waste DPS on the shielded Shrine.
4. Burst during Weakened: this is when the Shrine melts.
5. Repeat: the Shrine respawns in 60 seconds.
events.mothers_day_desc: A heartfelt seasonal celebration. Complete daily quests to earn Fragrance Points, exchange flowers in dedicated activities, and unlock cosmetic rewards inspired by motherhood and the language of flowers. Quests are revealed day by day, so check in regularly. Any unclaimed rewards are sent by mail when the event ends.
events.mothers_day_schedule_reset_value: Daily quests reset at 00:00 UTC. Each day's quests only start counting once they unlock.
events.mothers_day_activity_floral_desc: Complete daily and target quests to earn Floral Points. Common rewards unlock automatically when the event opens; higher-tier rewards are claimed as you accumulate points. Optional Advanced and Deluxe Pass Packs unlock extra reward tracks. Unused Floral Points expire when the event ends.
events.mothers_day_activity_exchange_desc: Collect Fragrant Bouquets during the event and trade them for Heartfelt Carnation Boxes. A simple, repeatable loop that builds up your draws for exclusive cosmetics.
events.mothers_day_activity_box_desc: Open Heartfelt Carnation Boxes to draw exclusive rewards. Each opening gives 1 point. Reach milestones to claim bonus rewards. Boxes also drop the limited 'Heart Petal' currency used in the Caring Market. Detailed drop rates are shown in-game via the Details button. Unused boxes are converted to 100 Credits each at the end of the event.
events.mothers_day_activity_surprise_desc: Open a flower box for a chance to win a bonus Heartfelt Carnation Box. A small extra layer of luck stacked on top of the main loop.
events.mothers_day_activity_market_desc: Spend the Heart Petals you collect through the event on hand-picked rewards. Be sure to spend them in time: leftover Heart Petals are converted to 10 Credits each at the end of the event.
events.mothers_day_cosmetic_skin_desc: An exclusive Flagship skin, available in personal and giftable versions. A flowing chain of light woven into a gentle cradle, evoking the origin of life and a mother's first touch.
events.mothers_day_cosmetic_kill_desc: A blooming kill effect, available in personal and giftable versions. Owning the effect grants a permanent +2% INT to all fleets.
events.mothers_day_cosmetic_frame_desc: A golden frame wrapped in carnations, awarded for participating in the 2026 Tender Letters event. Owning the frame grants a permanent +1% Defense to all fleets.
events.mothers_day_reward_carnations_value: Found during the event. Open one to randomly receive an Orange, Pink, or White Carnation, used in the Heartfelt Exchange. Unused Mysterious Carnations convert to 40,000 Galactic Coins each at the end. Orange = 100 Credits, Pink = 5,000 Water, White = 1,000 Metal.
events.mothers_day_reward_petals_value: Premium currency for the Caring Market. Unused Heart Petals convert to 10 Credits each at the end of the event.
events.mothers_day_reward_boxes_value: Used to draw exclusive rewards. Unused boxes convert to 100 Credits each at the end of the event.
events.mothers_day_protip_daily_desc: Daily quests only start counting from the moment they unlock, and don't carry over. Missing a day means losing those Fragrance Points. Set a quick reminder.
events.mothers_day_protip_carnations_title: Spend everything before the event ends
events.mothers_day_protip_carnations_desc: Any leftover event currency or item is converted into much smaller currency rewards once the event closes. Use the in-event exchange and shop to convert your stock into what you actually want before the deadline.
events.mothers_day_protip_gift_desc: Several of this event's rewards come in a giftable version. Coordinate with your guildmates so each item ends up with the player who benefits most from it.
events.childrens_day_desc: Celebrate Children's Day with a magical themed event! The special route leading to the Interstellar Amusement Park is now open to all commanders. This seasonal celebration offers multiple fun activities, strategic mini-games, and a wealth of exclusive cosmetic rewards to collect by earning event tokens.
events.childrens_day_activity_checkin_desc: A daily login calendar. Log in every day during the event to claim free rewards. Purchasing the Premium Pass unlocks a second track of enhanced rewards. Note that after the event concludes, unclaimed rewards can still be retrieved retroactively by spending Amber.
events.childrens_day_activity_bingo_desc: A quest system structured as a Bingo grid. Complete specific missions to clear rows and columns and unlock intermediate chests. Completing the entire grid grants the highly valuable Ultimate Bingo Chest containing the best rewards.
events.childrens_day_activity_battlepass_desc: Progress through this Pass by completing daily quests and long-term milestones to accumulate Childhood Points. The standard track is free for all players, while the Advanced and Deluxe passes (purchasable) grant access to premium cosmetic tiers and bonus resources.
events.childrens_day_activity_lottery_desc: Use your Little Dream Coins earned through gameplay or purchased packs to spin the prize wheel. Each spin brings you closer to outstanding rewards, including the coveted Park Souvenir Tickets. Every spin also contributes to your cumulative milestone points.
events.childrens_day_activity_chest_desc: A daily server-wide lottery taking place every evening at 22:00 UTC. Eligibility depends on your accumulated daily Purchase Points. Along with a chance at the grand prize, all qualified participants receive a participation reward.
events.childrens_day_activity_shop_desc: Redeem your hard-earned Park Souvenir Tickets in the exchange shop for exclusive park keepsakes and cosmetic items. Make sure to spend all your tickets before the event ends, as post-event automatic conversion rates into standard credits are highly unfavorable.
events.childrens_day_activity_defense_desc: A real-time strategy mini-game where you must defend the Castle of Wonder against incoming enemy waves. Earn gold by defeating enemies to purchase defense cards, recruit Champions, and deploy powerful buffs to secure your lines.
events.childrens_day_reward_skin1_title: Flagship Skin: Thief of Heavenly Fire
events.childrens_day_reward_skin1_desc: Exclusive legendary flagship skin, available in both personal and friendship versions.
events.childrens_day_reward_skin2_desc: Exclusive legendary flagship skin, available in both personal and friendship versions.
events.childrens_day_reward_frame_desc: Event-themed exclusive profile frame. Owning this frame increases the Defense of all Fleets by +1%.
events.childrens_day_reward_chat_desc: Event-themed chat frame unlocked during Children's Day.
events.childrens_day_reward_nameplate_desc: Event-themed nameplate unlocked during Children's Day.
events.childrens_day_protip_pass_desc: If you plan to purchase the Premium Pass, do so as early as possible to benefit from progression boosts from the very start and avoid missing out on daily chests.
events.childrens_day_protip_unused_desc: Unused Dream Coins and Souvenir Tickets will be automatically converted at a very low rate once the event closes. Plan your Souvenir Shop purchases carefully to clear your stock before the deadline.
events.childrens_day_protip_defense_desc: In the defense mini-game, prioritize champion variety and stacking synergistic buffs rather than just spamming units. A well-coordinated card setup is key to overcoming the tougher endless waves.
events.trade_route_defense_desc: Defend a trade convoy through 3 escalating phases to earn Blueprint - Opportunity and other rare flagship blueprints.
events.trade_route_defense_phases_desc: Phase 1: Concentrate on dealing rapid damage to swarm fleets and avoid assigning escorts. Phase 2: Distribute damage among shrines and medium threats; at most one escort is needed. Phase 3: A massive flagship spawns, requiring immediate full-team escort duty.
events.trade_route_defense_escort_desc: Select the highest difficulty level possible. Ship losses are temporary, but securing these rare rewards has permanent account value. Manual team coordination is superior to automated joining. Adjust escort rotation based on convoy durability.

champions.synergy_desc: It is <1>CRUCIAL</1> to match champions with the ship's Energy Type.
champions.golden_rule_desc: NEVER mix champion types. 3 Beams in a Beam ship is mandatory. A Kinetic champion in a Beam ship is useless.
champions.shard_management_desc: Save generic shards for <1>Lucius</1> or <1>Phade</1>. Absolutely avoid wasting them on Kama or Klara.
champions.update_note_desc: Damage is now more static and less reliant on RNG, but stat priorities remain the same.

desc.riian: Solid early game Kinetic attacker. Good single target damage.
desc.lani: Reliable Kinetic damage dealer. Easy to upgrade.
desc.cocoon: Incredible shields (20% Max HP) and durability. Top tier tank.
desc.eva: Core for Kinetic teams. Lowers enemy DEF by 20% to set up finishers.
desc.zora: Best Kinetic finisher. Deals massive burst damage (200% ATK).
desc.klara: Basic Beam healer. Good early sustain but falls off later.
desc.lucius: Strong Beam attacker. Prioritize if you lack Evan.
desc.doug: Consistent HOT (Heal Over Time) for Beam sustain.
desc.aliya: Beam Sync skill boosts ATK by 15%. Great opener for Beam teams.
desc.evan: Beam powerhouse. Piercing damage that scales to 150% ATK.
desc.kama: Decent Ion attacker can stun enemies.
desc.phade: Basic Ion healer. Useful for early Ion teams.
desc.jodi: Ramps team damage by 10% per skill use. Strong anchor for long fights.
desc.ajita: Essential for Ion rotations. Huge healing & team-wide INT buffs.
desc.lily: F2P friendly but S-Tier power. Great AOE and ability slowdowns.
desc.killer_bee: Elite Kinetic Attacker. Focuses on high physical damage.

champions.order_matters_desc: Position support champions such as Aliya, Eva, or Jodie in the initial slots of your flagship. Because slot abilities trigger sequentially, this sequencing ensures their stat enhancements are active when primary damage dealers execute their skills.
champions.cocoon_tooltip_warning_desc: The interface displays inaccurate values for Cocoon's healing and shielding stats. In live battles, rather than the stated 1500% shield and 240% heal, it actually provides a total absorption of approximately 110% to 150% of the defense rating.
champions.ultra_premium_desc: Top-tier endgame heroes like Evan Rogers, Killer Bee, and Lily offer exceptional power. While Evan and Bee are premium acquisitions and Lily requires a dedicated grind in Arena or Guild shops, they outperform fully upgraded epic heroes even at their lowest ranks (green star level).

flagships.title: Flagship Tactics
flagships.subtitle: Master your fleet with these advanced strategies.
flagships.vessels_vs_energy_desc: Do <1>not</1> confuse the 3 Flagship types with the 3 Damage types (Beam/Ion/Kinetic). Any Damage type can be assigned to any Ship via the <3>Core Component</3>.
flagships.starting_energy_desc: Energy gains from Promotion ranks 5 and 6 <1>stack</1>.
flagships.type_choice_desc: Although flexible, each ship ends up best complementing the default energy type of its Legendary champions in the late game.
flagships.acquisition_desc: Prioritize <1>Top 100</1> events for Gram Blueprints and <3>PVP/GvG</3> for Opportunity.

decks.title: Meta Ship Decks
decks.subtitle: Optimized fleet compositions for maximum efficiency.

gram.title: Gram
gram.subtitle: The Indestructible Fortress
gram.strategy_early: Use as a main tank to soak damage while your fleet scales.
gram.strategy_late: Transitions to a specialized buffer for Kinetic fleets.
gram.strategy_use: Best used with <0>Kinetic</0> fleets due to its synergy with physical damage reduction.
gram.ability1: <0>Titan Plating</0>: Reduces incoming damage by a flat amount + percentage.
gram.ability2: <0>Fleet Command</0>: Passively boosts the HP of all allied ships.

opportunity.title: Opportunity
opportunity.subtitle: The Tactical Assassin
opportunity.strategic_note: Its <0>Evasion</0> stat allows it to completely dodge attacks, making it deceptively tanky if built correctly.
opportunity.ability1_op: <0>Phantom Drive</0>: Increases evasion by 25% for short durations.
opportunity.ability2_op: <0>Precision Strike</0>: Guarantees a critical hit on the lowest HP enemy.

demerzel.title: Demerzel
demerzel.subtitle: The Ultimate Weapon
demerzel.strengths: Raw Firepower, Area of Effect, Shield Breaking
demerzel.strategy_synergy: Synergizes perfectly with <0>Beam</0> and <2>Ion</2> teams to strip shields or stun.
demerzel.ability1_dem: <0>Doomsday Laser</0>: Massive AOE damage that ignores 50% of shields.
demerzel.ability2_dem: <0>Energy Siphon</0>: Drains enemy energy to recharge its own main weapon.

gungir.title: Gungir
gungir.subtitle: Pace Controller
gungir.strengths: High energy generation, opponent suppression, and scaling combat boosts.
gungir.strategy_1: Accelerate your champion skill cycle while suppressing the enemy's ability speed.
gungir.strategy_2: Scales to 25% after two full skill rotations, outperforming other options in longer engagements.
gungir.ability2_desc: Drains a flat 300 energy from the opponent ship, delaying their ability rotations.

flagships.deployment_whales_kinetic: Kinetic -> Gungir (top offensive setup with Killer Bee)
flagships.deployment_f2p_kinetic: Kinetic -> Gram (main combat ship, accessible blueprints)
flagships.deployment_f2p_ion: Ion -> Opportunity (balanced trade and burst damage)

ground_guide.title: Ground Teams
ground_guide.subtitle: Optimal team compositions for ground combat.

builder_ui.title: Tools
builder_ui.how_it_works_desc:
The game calculates build times using this formula:
<1>(Base - Flat) / (1 + Bonus%)

Flat reductions (Crew Cabins) are applied <4>before percentage multipliers, making them extremely valuable for short builds but less impactful for week-long upgrades.

daily_checklist.title: Daily Tasks
daily_checklist.subtitle: Track your daily objectives. All progress resets at 00:00 UTC.
daily_checklist.reset_confirm: Are you sure you want to reset all your daily tasks?

login_modal.need_register: Need to register a new account?
login_modal.magic_link_sent: Magic link sent. Check your inbox.
login_modal.error_weak_password: Password should be at least 6 characters.
login_modal.error_auth_failed: Failed to authenticate. Please try again.
login_modal.error_google_failed: Failed to log in with Google.

tools_ui.manual_tip: 💡 Tip: The database time is not verified for this level. We recommend entering the exact game time manually for accurate results.
tools_ui.crew_post_init_desc: The game recalculates total speed including crew bonus after initialization.
tools_ui.data_credit: Data collection powered by the incredible efforts of
tools_ui.select_range: Select a level range to view breakdown
tools_ui.tile_intro: Select a calculator to plan your progression.
tools_ui.tile_build_time_desc: Calculate building upgrade times with cabin reductions and crew bonuses.
tools_ui.tile_champion_upgrade_desc: Plan champion shard costs from current to target level.
tools_ui.tile_nexus_desc: Estimate Comp. Mods, Echoes and levels for your weapon Nexus.
tools_ui.tile_gvg_desc: Track Galactic Coins and Computational Components needs across all GvG trees.
tools_ui.tile_combat_craft_desc: Total resource costs across the full Combat Craft tech tree.

gift_codes.title: Gift Codes
gift_codes.subtitle: Active gift codes for Foundation: Galactic Frontier.
gift_codes.no_codes: No active codes at the moment. Check back soon!
gift_codes.tagline: Redeem these codes in-game for free rewards.
gift_codes.redeem_intro: Click the copy icon next to a code below.
gift_codes.redeem_steps: In-game, tap your <1>avatar</1> (top left), select <3>"Settings"</3>, then tap <5>"Gift Code"</5> and paste.
gift_codes.community_thanks: A big thank you to the amazing community members who help keep this list updated.

nexus.title: Nexus Specialization
nexus.select_higher_target: Select a target stage higher than your current stage to see the cost.
